title: oxalis smalliana
intro: Oxalis smalliana, also known as the woodland sorrel or small wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species within the oxalis genus that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a natural diuretic and to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and gout due to its anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of oxalis smalliana include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve digestion, and act as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Oxalis smalliana can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it is also used as a food source, with its leaves and flowers being edible and used in salads.
